# Kubernetes Lifecycle

Load images, bootstrap or join kubeadm nodes, and verify Kubernetes-specific cluster state.

## Summary

- group: `kubernetes-lifecycle`
- kinds: `LoadImage`, `VerifyImage`, `InitKubeadm`, `JoinKubeadm`, `UpgradeKubeadm`, `ResetKubeadm`, `CheckKubernetesCluster`

## When To Use This Group

Use this group for kubeadm bootstrap, join, reset, upgrade, image loading, and Kubernetes cluster verification steps.

## Typical Flows

### Bootstrap a control plane

- kinds: `LoadImage`, `InitKubeadm`, `CheckKubernetesCluster`

### Join workers

- kinds: `JoinKubeadm`, `CheckKubernetesCluster`
- note: Final cluster verification usually runs only on the control-plane role.

## Shared Step Fields

Shared step envelope fields such as `id`, `apiVersion`, `kind`, `when`, `retry`, `timeout`, `register`, and `metadata` are documented in [Workflow Schema](../schema/workflow.md).

## Supported Kinds

- `LoadImage`: Load prepared image archives into the local container runtime.
- `VerifyImage`: Verify that required container images already exist on the node.
- `InitKubeadm`: Run kubeadm init and write the join command to a file.
- `JoinKubeadm`: Run kubeadm join for a worker or additional control-plane node.
- `UpgradeKubeadm`: Run kubeadm upgrade apply and optional kubelet restart.
- `ResetKubeadm`: Run kubeadm reset and optional cleanup steps.
- `CheckKubernetesCluster`: Poll and verify Kubernetes cluster health on the local node.

## `LoadImage`

Load prepared image archives into the local container runtime.


### When To Use

Use this during apply before verifying or using images from an offline bundle.

### Example

```yaml
kind: LoadImage
spec:
  sourceDir: images/control-plane
  runtime: ctr
  images:
    - registry.k8s.io/kube-apiserver:v1.30.1
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.command` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Optional runtime command override. | `[ctr,-n,k8s.io,images,import,{archive}]` |
| `spec.images` | `array<string>` | yes | `` | `` | Image references to load from the prepared archives. | `[registry.k8s.io/kube-apiserver:v1.30.1]` |
| `spec.runtime` | `string` | no | `` | `auto, ctr, docker, podman` | Runtime loader to use for imports. | `ctr` |
| `spec.sourceDir` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Directory containing prepared image archives. | `images/control-plane` |

### Notes

- `command` may include `{archive}` placeholders that deck substitutes per image archive.

## `VerifyImage`

Verify that required container images already exist on the node.


### When To Use

Use this during apply when images should already be present and only need verification.

### Example

```yaml
kind: VerifyImage
spec:
  command: [ctr, -n, k8s.io, images, list, -q]
  images:
    - registry.k8s.io/kube-apiserver:v1.30.1
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.command` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Optional image-listing command override. | `[ctr,-n,k8s.io,images,list,-q]` |
| `spec.images` | `array<string>` | yes | `` | `` | Image references that must already exist in the runtime. | `[registry.k8s.io/kube-apiserver:v1.30.1]` |

### Notes

- Use this instead of `LoadImage` when the runtime is expected to be pre-populated.

## `InitKubeadm`

Run kubeadm init and write the join command to a file.

- outputs: `joinFile`

### When To Use

Use this to bootstrap a control-plane node after host prerequisites are ready.

### Example

```yaml
kind: InitKubeadm
spec:
  outputJoinFile: /tmp/deck/join.txt
  podNetworkCIDR: 10.244.0.0/16
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.advertiseAddress` | `string` | no | `` | `` | API server advertise address or `auto`. | `auto` |
| `spec.configFile` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Path to an explicit kubeadm config file. | `/tmp/deck/kubeadm.conf` |
| `spec.configTemplate` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Inline kubeadm config template or `default`. | `default` |
| `spec.criSocket` | `string` | no | `` | `` | CRI socket path passed to kubeadm. | `unix:///run/containerd/containerd.sock` |
| `spec.extraArgs` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Additional flags passed directly to kubeadm. | `[--skip-phases=addon/kube-proxy]` |
| `spec.ignorePreflightErrors` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Preflight checks to suppress. | `[swap]` |
| `spec.kubernetesVersion` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Kubernetes version string passed to kubeadm. | `v1.30.1` |
| `spec.outputJoinFile` | `string` | yes | `` | `` | Path where the generated join command is written after init. | `/tmp/deck/join.txt` |
| `spec.podNetworkCIDR` | `string` | no | `` | `` | CIDR range for the pod network. | `10.244.0.0/16` |
| `spec.skipIfAdminConfExists` | `boolean` | no | `true` | `` | Skip init if `/etc/kubernetes/admin.conf` already exists. | `true` |

### Notes

- When `skipIfAdminConfExists` skips the step, deck does not create a new join artifact unless the file already exists.
- Load prepared control-plane images with `LoadImage` before bootstrap rather than relying on kubeadm image pulls.

## `JoinKubeadm`

Run kubeadm join for a worker or additional control-plane node.


### When To Use

Use this after a bootstrap node has produced a valid join file or config.

### Example

```yaml
kind: JoinKubeadm
spec:
  configFile: /tmp/deck/kubeadm-join.yaml
  extraArgs: [--skip-phases=preflight]
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.asControlPlane` | `boolean` | no | `false` | `` | Join as an additional control-plane member instead of a worker. | `false` |
| `spec.configFile` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Path to an explicit kubeadm join config file. | `/tmp/deck/kubeadm-join.yaml` |
| `spec.extraArgs` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Additional flags passed directly to kubeadm join. | `[--skip-phases=preflight]` |
| `spec.joinFile` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Path to the join command file produced by a prior init run. | `/tmp/deck/join.txt` |

### Validation Rules

- Exactly one of `spec.joinFile` or `spec.configFile` must be set.

### Notes

- Provide exactly one of `joinFile` or `configFile`.

## `UpgradeKubeadm`

Run kubeadm upgrade apply and optional kubelet restart.


### When To Use

Use this to upgrade a local kubeadm-managed control-plane node with a typed workflow step.

### Example

```yaml
kind: UpgradeKubeadm
spec:
  kubernetesVersion: v1.31.0
  ignorePreflightErrors: [Swap]
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.extraArgs` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Additional flags passed directly to kubeadm upgrade. | `[--allow-experimental-upgrades]` |
| `spec.ignorePreflightErrors` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Preflight checks to suppress during upgrade. | `[Swap]` |
| `spec.kubeletService` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Service name restarted after a successful upgrade. | `kubelet` |
| `spec.kubernetesVersion` | `string` | yes | `` | `` | Kubernetes version string passed to kubeadm upgrade. | `v1.31.0` |
| `spec.restartKubelet` | `boolean` | no | `true` | `` | Restart kubelet after a successful upgrade. | `true` |

### Notes

- Restart kubelet after a successful upgrade unless a separate service step owns that lifecycle.

## `ResetKubeadm`

Run kubeadm reset and optional cleanup steps.


### When To Use

Use this to tear down an existing kubeadm-managed node safely.

### Example

```yaml
kind: ResetKubeadm
spec:
  force: true
  removePaths: [/etc/cni/net.d, /var/lib/etcd]
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.cleanupContainers` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Containers to stop and remove during cleanup. | `[kube-apiserver,etcd]` |
| `spec.criSocket` | `string` | no | `` | `` | CRI socket path passed to kubeadm. | `unix:///run/containerd/containerd.sock` |
| `spec.extraArgs` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Additional flags passed directly to kubeadm reset. | `[--skip-phases=preflight]` |
| `spec.force` | `boolean` | no | `false` | `` | Pass `--force` to kubeadm reset. | `true` |
| `spec.ignoreErrors` | `boolean` | no | `false` | `` | Continue with cleanup even if kubeadm reset itself fails. | `true` |
| `spec.removeFiles` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Files to delete during cleanup. | `[/etc/kubernetes/admin.conf]` |
| `spec.removePaths` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Directories to delete during cleanup. | `[/etc/cni/net.d,/var/lib/etcd]` |
| `spec.reportFile` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Optional reset report file written after cleanup. | `/tmp/deck/reports/reset-state.txt` |
| `spec.reportResetReason` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Value written into the reset report as `resetReason`. | `node-reset-acceptance` |
| `spec.restartRuntimeService` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Runtime service to restart after cleanup. | `containerd` |
| `spec.stopKubelet` | `boolean` | no | `true` | `` | Stop kubelet before running reset. | `true` |
| `spec.stopKubeletAfterReset` | `boolean` | no | `` | `` | Stop kubelet again after runtime verification completes. | `true` |
| `spec.verifyContainersAbsent` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Containers that must no longer exist after cleanup. | `[kube-apiserver,etcd]` |
| `spec.waitForMissingManifestsGlob` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Glob that must resolve to zero matches before the step succeeds. | `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/*.yaml` |
| `spec.waitForRuntimeReady` | `boolean` | no | `` | `` | Poll runtime readiness after cleanup. | `true` |
| `spec.waitForRuntimeService` | `boolean` | no | `` | `` | Wait until the runtime service reports active. | `true` |

### Notes

- This step focuses on cleanup and convergence after kubeadm reset.

## `CheckKubernetesCluster`

Poll and verify Kubernetes cluster health on the local node.


### When To Use

Use this for typed bootstrap and upgrade verification instead of ad-hoc kubectl shell loops.

### Example

```yaml
kind: CheckKubernetesCluster
spec:
  interval: 5s
  nodes:
    total: 1
    ready: 1
    controlPlaneReady: 1
  reports:
    nodesPath: /tmp/deck/reports/bootstrap-nodes.txt
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.fileAssertions` | `array<object>` | no | `` | `` | Optional file-content assertions evaluated on every poll attempt. | `[{path:/etc/containerd/config.toml,contains:[registry.k8s.io/pause:3.10]}]` |
| `spec.initialDelay` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Optional delay before the first poll attempt. | `10s` |
| `spec.interval` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Duration between poll attempts while waiting for cluster state to converge. | `5s` |
| `spec.kubeSystem` | `object` | no | `` | `` | Optional checks for kube-system pod readiness. | `{readyNames:[etcd-control-plane]}` |
| `spec.kubeconfig` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Kubeconfig path used for kubectl-based checks. | `/etc/kubernetes/admin.conf` |
| `spec.nodes` | `object` | no | `` | `` | Optional checks for cluster node count and readiness. | `{total:1,ready:1,controlPlaneReady:1}` |
| `spec.reports` | `object` | no | `` | `` | Optional paths for writing node and cluster state reports. | `{nodesPath:/tmp/deck/reports/bootstrap-nodes.txt}` |
| `spec.timeout` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Maximum total duration to keep polling before the step fails. | `10m` |
| `spec.versions` | `object` | no | `` | `` | Optional checks for Kubernetes component versions. | `{server:v1.31.0,kubelet:v1.31.0}` |

### Nested Objects

### `spec.kubeSystem`

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.kubeSystem.jsonReportPath` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Optional JSON report path for `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o json`. | `/tmp/deck/reports/kube-system-pods.json` |
| `spec.kubeSystem.readyNames` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Exact kube-system pod names that must be present and fully Ready. | `[etcd-control-plane,kube-apiserver-control-plane]` |
| `spec.kubeSystem.readyPrefixMinimums` | `array<object>` | no | `` | `` | Prefix-based readiness requirements with minimum Ready pod counts. | `[{prefix:coredns-,minReady:2}]` |
| `spec.kubeSystem.readyPrefixes` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Pod-name prefixes for which at least one matching Ready pod must exist. | `[kube-proxy-]` |
| `spec.kubeSystem.reportPath` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Optional text report path for `kubectl get pods -n kube-system`. | `/tmp/deck/reports/kube-system-pods.txt` |

### `spec.nodes`

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.nodes.controlPlaneReady` | `integer` | no | `` | `` | Expected count of Ready control-plane nodes. | `1` |
| `spec.nodes.ready` | `integer` | no | `` | `` | Expected count of Ready nodes. | `1` |
| `spec.nodes.total` | `integer` | no | `` | `` | Expected total node count returned by `kubectl get nodes`. | `1` |

### `spec.reports`

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.reports.clusterNodesPath` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Optional second node report path for shared cluster node reports. | `/tmp/deck/reports/cluster-nodes.txt` |
| `spec.reports.nodesPath` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Optional report file path for `kubectl get nodes` output. | `/tmp/deck/reports/bootstrap-nodes.txt` |

### `spec.versions`

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.versions.kubeadm` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Expected local kubeadm version from `kubeadm version -o short`. | `v1.31.0` |
| `spec.versions.kubelet` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Expected kubelet version for the selected node. | `v1.31.0` |
| `spec.versions.nodeName` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Node name used when reading kubelet version. | `control-plane` |

| `spec.versions.server` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Expected API server version from `kubectl version -o json`. | `v1.31.0` |
| `spec.versions.targetVersion` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Target Kubernetes version written into the optional version report file. | `v1.31.0` |
